title: Sedition vs Freedom of Speech
description: In August 2024, Arvind Rao, a university student in Bangalore, posted a series of social media messages and delivered a speech at a campus protest criticizing government policies and calling for large-scale public dissent. The posts went viral, following which police registered a case under sedition law, alleging that his statements incited disaffection against the government. Arvind challenged the charges, claiming his speech was protected under freedom of expression and did not incite violence.

characters.name: Arvind Rao
characters.details: 22, university student, accused of posting controversial content

characters.name: Inspector Nagesh Kumar
characters.details: Police officer who registered the FIR

characters.name: Priya Menon
characters.details: Student present at the protest, supports petitioner’s version

characters.name: Rohit Shetty
characters.details: Attendee claiming speech created unrest among crowd

evidence: Expert opinion on interpretation of speech
evidence_chain: Petitioner publishes posts and delivers speech
Content criticizes government and calls for dissent
Police interpret speech as inciting disaffection
Petitioner claims no incitement to violence
Witnesses provide conflicting accounts of impact
Court evaluates intent, content, and effect under Article 19(2)
legal_ambiguity: Whether strong criticism of the government and calls for dissent amount to sedition or remain protected speech absent incitement to violence.
